response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack engineer with a genuine track record in distributed systems and engineering craft (TDD, CI/CD), but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience as a hard minimum; the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with C++ listed at the tail end of a skills section otherwise dominated by JavaScript/TypeScript. There is no demonstrated cloud platform engineering at the depth required (AWS CDK usage in a pricing team and a personal Kubernetes project do not constitute 5 years of large-scale cloud infrastructure), and no evidence of formal technical leadership over cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Strong distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-throughput service design.
Consistent engineering-craft advocacy across career: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pair programming, and community knowledge-sharing (workshops, talks).
Long-tenured open-source and platform ownership (Northwind Labs GIS platform on Kubernetes, multiple D-language libraries) demonstrating ability to drive projects independently.
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — the role requires 8 years; the resume shows roughly 6 months (National Instruments, 2014–2015) with no subsequent C++ work in over a decade.

Cloud infrastructure depth is shallow for a platform leadership role — AWS CDK in a product pricing team and a personal Kubernetes deployment do not satisfy 5 years of large-scale cloud/distributed infrastructure.

No demonstrated technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineers; staff and senior titles appear scoped to individual contribution and craft mentorship rather than platform-level architectural ownership.

key_factors.factor: Engineering craft and distributed systems exposure
